label: Discord Bot

tools.name: AddReaction
tools.description: Add an emoji reaction to a Discord message as the bot.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the message to react to.

parameters.name: emoji
parameters.description: The emoji to add: a unicode emoji character, or a custom emoji as name:id.

tools.name: DeleteMessage
tools.description: Delete a message from a Discord channel. This cannot be undone.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the message to delete.

tools.name: EditMessage
tools.description:
Edit a message the bot posted. The bot can only edit its own messages.

Edits suppress @everyone, @here, and role mentions, so correcting an earlier post never
pings the whole server (Discord re-parses mentions in edited content by default).

Editing a message authored by someone else is not an error: it returns a result whose
``status`` is ``not_author`` so the caller can pivot (e.g. post a new message) without
its tool chain aborting.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the bot's message to edit.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The new message text (1-2000 characters).

content.value: ✅ The scheduled maintenance has been completed. All services are back online as of 3:45 PM UTC.

tools.name: GetChannel
tools.description:
Get a single Discord channel's name, type, and topic.

A missing channel (e.g. one that was deleted) is not an error: the result's ``status``
is ``not_found`` so the caller can branch without a try/catch. A genuine permission
problem is still raised.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel or thread id to fetch.

tools.name: GetMessage
tools.description:
Fetch a single Discord message by id.

A missing message (e.g. one that was deleted) is not an error: the result's ``status``
is ``not_found`` so the caller can branch without a try/catch. A genuine permission
problem is still raised.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel or thread id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The message id to fetch.

tools.name: GetServer
tools.description:
Get a single Discord server's name, owner, and member count.

A server the bot cannot resolve (it is not a member, or the id is wrong) is not an
error: the result's ``status`` is ``not_found`` so the caller can branch without a
try/catch. A genuine permission problem is still raised.

parameters.name: server_id
parameters.description: The server (guild) id to fetch.

tools.name: GetUser
tools.description:
Resolve a user id (such as a message author_id) into a human-readable name.

Pass server_id to fold in the user's nickname in that server. Use this to turn the opaque
author_id on messages into a name without leaving the agent. A user id that resolves to no
account is not an error: the result's ``status`` is ``not_found`` so the caller can branch.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The user id to resolve, such as a message author_id.

parameters.name: server_id
parameters.description: Resolve the user's nickname within this server (guild). Leave empty to return only the account-level profile.

tools.name: ListActiveThreads
tools.description: List a Discord server's currently active (non-archived) threads.

parameters.name: server_id
parameters.description: The server (guild) id whose active threads to list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum threads to return (1-100). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position into the thread list. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListChannels
tools.description: List the channels in a Discord server, optionally filtered by type.

parameters.name: server_id
parameters.description: The server (guild) id whose channels to list.

parameters.name: channel_type
parameters.description: Restrict results to one channel type. Defaults to ALL (no filter).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum channels to return (1-100). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position into the filtered list. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListMessages
tools.description: Read a channel's or thread's recent messages, newest first.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel or thread id to read. A thread is a channel, so this reads threads too.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum messages to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Return only messages older than this message id. Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return only messages newer than this message id. Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: around
parameters.description: Return messages centered on this message id. Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: include_system
parameters.description: Include Discord's auto-generated system notices (thread-created, pin, join messages). Defaults to false, which keeps a catch-up read free of blank non-conversational rows.

tools.name: ListPinnedMessages
tools.description: List the pinned messages in a Discord channel.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id whose pinned messages to list.

tools.name: ListServers
tools.description: List the Discord servers the bot is a member of and can act in.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum servers to return (1-200). Defaults to 200.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Return servers before this server id (for paging backward). Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return servers after this server id; pass next_cursor to get the next page. Leave empty to start at the first page.

output.description: The servers the bot belongs to.

tools.name: PinMessage
tools.description: Pin a message in a Discord channel so the team can find it later.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the message to pin.

tools.name: RemoveReaction
tools.description:
Remove the bot's own emoji reaction from a Discord message.

Removes only the bot's reaction; other users' reactions with the same emoji are
unaffected. Removing a reaction the bot never added is a no-op that returns
``removed`` False, so the caller can tell that case apart from a real removal.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the message to remove the reaction from.

parameters.name: emoji
parameters.description: The emoji to remove: a unicode emoji character, or a custom emoji as name:id.

tools.name: ReplyToMessage
tools.description:
Reply to an existing Discord message so the answer stays tied to the question.

To reply inside a thread, pass the thread id as channel_id and a message id from
inside the thread as message_id. A thread's own id is its starter message, which lives
in the parent channel, not inside the thread.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the original message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the message to reply to.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The reply text (1-2000 characters).

parameters.name: mention_author
parameters.description: Whether to ping the author of the message being replied to. Defaults to false.

content.value: Thanks for your question! The event starts at 3 PM UTC this Saturday. Feel free to ask if you need more details.

tools.name: SearchMessages
tools.description:
Find messages by text and/or author in one channel or across a whole server.

Use a single call with server_id to answer "I remember a message but not where" without
looping channels yourself; a server-wide search covers text and announcement channels plus
active threads and forum posts. Discord's bot API has no server-side message search, so
this scans recent messages locally and covers the most recent ``max_scan`` messages, not
full history. Matching reads ``content``, which is empty for messages the bot cannot see
in full without the Message Content intent.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel or thread id to search (a thread is a channel, so this searches threads too). Leave empty to search a whole server instead via server_id. Provide exactly one of channel_id or server_id.

parameters.name: server_id
parameters.description: The server (guild) id to search across all of its text and announcement channels. Leave empty to search a single channel via channel_id. Provide exactly one of channel_id or server_id.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Text to find in message content (case-insensitive). Leave empty to match any content, which is useful when filtering only by author_id.

parameters.name: author_id
parameters.description: Only return messages posted by this author id. Leave empty to match any author. Combine with an empty query to list a user's recent messages.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum matching messages to return (1-50). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: max_scan
parameters.description: Maximum recent messages to scan while searching (50-1000). Defaults to 300. In a server-wide search this is the total budget shared across channels. Raise to look further back at the cost of more API calls.

tools.name: SendMessage
tools.description:
Post a new message to a Discord channel as the bot.

Posts with @everyone, @here, and role mentions suppressed so a routine update never
pings the whole server.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id to post to.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The message text to post (1-2000 characters).

content.value: 📢 Server maintenance is scheduled for tonight at 11:00 PM UTC. Expected downtime is approximately 30 minutes. Thank you for your patience!

tools.name: StartThread
tools.description:
Start a thread from a message, standalone in a text channel, or as a forum/media post.

Discord seeds forum and media posts from the create call but does not accept an opening
body for text-channel threads, so for those this posts ``content`` as a follow-up opening
message in one call. Mentions in ``content`` are suppressed so seeding never pings the
server.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id to start the thread in.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The thread name (1-100 characters).

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: Start the thread attached to this existing message. Leave empty to start a standalone thread in the channel.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Body that seeds the thread (1-2000 characters). For a forum or media channel this opens the post and is required. For a standalone text-channel thread it is posted as the thread's opening message. Ignored when starting the thread from an existing message. Leave empty to open an unseeded text-channel thread.

parameters.name: auto_archive_minutes
parameters.description: Inactivity period before Discord auto-archives the thread. Defaults to ONE_DAY.

content.value: Welcome to this week's discussion thread! Feel free to share your thoughts and ideas below.

tools.name: UnpinMessage
tools.description: Unpin a previously pinned message in a Discord channel.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: The channel id the message is in.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The id of the message to unpin.

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description:
Return the authenticated Discord bot's own user identity.

Use this to verify the configured bot token and confirm which bot account the tools
act as. It is a verification helper, not a required preamble to the other tools.

summary:
Discord Bot toolkit lets an Arcade agent act as a Discord bot — reading and writing messages, managing threads, reacting, pinning, and introspecting servers, channels, and users through the Discord Bot API.

## Capabilities

- **Messaging** — send, edit, delete, reply to, search, and paginate messages in channels and threads; mention suppression is enforced on send, edit, and thread seeding so agents never accidentally ping `@everyone` or roles.
- **Reactions & pins** — add/remove the bot's emoji reactions (non-destructive: removing a reaction never added is a safe no-op); pin and unpin messages in any accessible channel.
- **Threads & forums** — list active threads, start threads from messages or as standalone text-channel threads, and create forum/media posts in a single call.
- **Discovery & introspection** — list servers the bot belongs to, list and filter channels by type, list pinned messages, resolve user IDs to human-readable names (with per-server nickname support), and verify bot identity via `WhoAmI`.
- **Graceful not-found handling** — missing messages, channels, servers, and users return a structured `not_found` status rather than raising errors, letting agent chains branch cleanly without try/catch.

## Secrets

`DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` — The bot token issued by Discord that authenticates every API call. To obtain it:
1. Open the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) and select (or create) your application.
2. Go to **Bot** in the left sidebar. Under **Token**, click **Reset Token** (or **Copy** if shown) to retrieve the secret value — it is only displayed once after a reset.
3. Ensure the bot has the **Message Content** intent enabled (**Privileged Gateway Intents** section on the same page) if you need `content` populated on messages the bot did not send; without it, `content` is empty for those messages and `SearchMessages` coverage is limited.
4. Invite the bot to your server(s) with appropriate permissions (at minimum: `Read Messages/View Channels`, `Send Messages`, `Manage Messages` for pin/delete, `Add Reactions`).

Store the token in Arcade using the [Arcade secrets guide](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) or directly at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
